Add mise test job and pyrefly type checker to act.yaml and cookiecutter template (#8)

### Background and Links
* The `test` job (`mise test` → `pytest --cov`) was missing from both
the repo's own workflow and the cookiecutter template it generates.
Additionally, the workflow name was not set, `basedpyright` was in
`pre-commit-fast`, and Meta's v2 type checker (`pyrefly`) was not
configured.

### Changes and Testing
* Added `test` job to `.github/workflows/act.yaml` and
`{{cookiecutter.dot}}/.github/workflows/act.yaml`, matching the existing
`check`/`tabularize` job structure: checkout → `jdx/mise-action@v3` →
`mise test`
* Added `name: act` to both act.yaml files so the workflow displays as
"act" in the GitHub Actions UI
* Removed `basedpyright` from the `pre-commit-fast` task group in both
`mise.toml` files; the `[tasks.basedpyright]` definition is retained for
manual use
* Added `[tasks.pyrefly]` (`pyrefly check`) to both `mise.toml` files
(root symlinks to `{{cookiecutter.dot}}/mise.toml`) and placed it in
`pre-commit-fast`
* Added `pyrefly` to `pyproject.toml` optional deps and
`pyrefly==0.60.2` to `requirements.txt` so `uv pip sync` installs it
before CI invokes the task
* Added `run = 'git diff --color=always --exit-code && echo "No
changes"'` to `[tasks.pre-commit-fast]` and `[tasks.pre-commit-all]`:
shows a colorized diff of any files changed by formatters, exits
non-zero to fail CI if anything was modified, and prints "No changes"
when the tree is clean

### Questions and Followup
* Should `test` depend on `check` passing first, or remain independent
(current)?

---------

commit.message:
fix: prevent false track-change on queue exhaustion echo

Keep _current_streaming_track_id set during the post-advance wait
period. Previously it was cleared immediately after the stream ended,
so when Ynison echoed back the completion signal (same track_id),
_activate_playback saw track_id != None and treated it as a new track.
This caused the SAME track to restart at position=duration (end),
finish instantly, and loop until timeout.

Now the echo is correctly recognized as the same track (no-op in
_activate_playback), and only a genuinely new track from the YM
radio engine triggers _track_changed_event.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Assumed median scores for non-voting players (#15)

When voting closes with some players not having voted on certain songs,
scores are imbalanced. This adds median-based assumed scores for missing
votes so every song is scored by every player.

### Scoring logic

- `computeMedianScore(scores)` — median of actual votes, `Math.floor`
for even-length arrays
- `scoreToEmoji(score)` — rounds down to the largest emoji score ≤ the
computed median
- Both live in `src/app/shared/scoring.ts` as pure functions with 13
unit tests

```ts
computeMedianScore([5, 3])  // → 4
scoreToEmoji(4)              // → "😁" (score 3, the largest ≤ 4)
```

### Data layer (`data.ts`)

- `SongResult.voteBreakdown` entries gain an `assumed: boolean` field
- `getResultsByScore` now fetches voters, identifies who didn't vote per
song, and fills in assumed votes using the median — these count toward
`totalScore`
- New `getAssumedVotesForVoter(gameId, voterName)` returns assumed
ratings for the vote page

### Vote page

- When game is closed, unrated songs show the assumed emoji with a
dashed border, 50% opacity, and an "(assumed)" label
- No visual change for songs the voter actually rated

**Bob's vote page — mix of actual votes (Moldova, Sweden) and assumed
votes (Croatia onward):**

![Bob's vote page with mixed actual and
assumed](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/d620a1ea-ca6c-452b-ad1d-806fae63adb2)

### Results reveal

- Actual and assumed votes are separated in the breakdown
- Assumed votes render under an "Assumed (didn't vote):" sub-heading
with dashed border styling
- Section is hidden when all players voted

**Results reveal — Sweden scored by Alice & Bob, with Charlie's assumed
median vote:**

![Results reveal with assumed
votes](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/efdd26a4-373e-4ae7-95d0-cf823c3e3856)

### Infrastructure

- Added `vitest` (separate `vitest.config.ts` to avoid Cloudflare Vite
plugin conflict) with `pnpm run test:unit`

commit.message:
fix: keep radio session alive on queue exhaustion

- Send paused=False (not True) in completion signal so the YM app
  radio engine stays active and pushes more tracks
- Wait 30s in the stream loop for new tracks instead of stopping
  immediately — gives the controller time to populate the queue
- Only set _stream_stop_event after timeout, not on exhaustion

The previous paused=True caused the YM app to end the radio session,
preventing new tracks from being pushed. With paused=False, the device
appears 'still active' and the radio engine keeps working.

commit.message:
Fix reveal brush: always update document dirty signal + add ghost layer tests

Bug: RevealBrush.stop() was intercepted by the ghost-mode branch in
ClickAndDragTool.stop(), which never calls document_dirty_signal.value++.
This meant revealed pixels were written to the document canvas but the
Three.js texture was never updated — so reveals were invisible on screen.

Fix: RevealBrush overrides stop() to always go through the normal commit
path (begin_undo_capture → commit_to_document → dirty++ → push_undo).

Also adds tests/test_ghost_layer.mjs with 7 unit tests covering:
- reveal_ghost_to_document copies + consumes ghost pixels
- only covered pixels are affected
- existing doc pixels are preserved
- tool_to_ghost_canvas writes actual colors
- tool_erase_ghost_canvas clears ghost pixels
- no-op when ghost canvas is empty
- incremental reveals work correctly across multiple strokes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
